rationale: The feature matrix directly includes a future return created with a negative shift, so the model can see the prediction horizon.
The next-day return is shifted backward and used as an input feature, leaking future price movement into training.
The trading signal is generated from a future volume change that would not be known at decision time, and the reported strategy return is gross of trading costs.
The shifted future return is both the basis for the target and included in the feature list, creating target leakage.
The z-score uses full-sample mean and standard deviation before train/test separation, so test-period distribution information leaks into training.
StandardScaler is fit on the full dataset before chronological splitting, which contaminates the training transformation.
The min and max values are computed on all rows before the test set is separated, leaking future extrema.
The market-cap z-score is computed using full-sample statistics before the date cutoff is applied.
A shuffled train_test_split breaks the chronological structure needed for a financial time-series evaluation.
Sorting by date is undone by a shuffled random split, so training can include observations after test observations.
The return classifier uses a shuffled train/test split on time-indexed market observations.
The backtest evaluation randomly divides dated rows, which invalidates chronological out-of-sample testing.
The backtest reports strategy returns and Sharpe ratio without fees, slippage, commissions, or turnover costs.
The strategy annualizes gross returns without modeling trading frictions.
A high-turnover signal is evaluated with gross strategy returns and no cost adjustment.
A transaction cost variable is declared but never subtracted from strategy returns, making this an intentional subtle failure case.
The writeup claims state-of-the-art performance and omits baselines, so the conclusion is not supported by evidence.
The writeup asserts outperformance from a chart without providing a baseline table or statistical support.
The writeup generalizes from a short BTC sample to live trading superiority without baselines or robustness checks.
The signal uses lagged information, applies an explicit turnover cost, and makes no unsupported performance claim.
The scaler is fit only on the training window and then applied to train and test separately.
The split preserves chronological order and does not use future features or global normalization.
The report frames the sample descriptively, includes a baseline reference, and avoids exaggerated unsupported claims.
The strategy uses lagged inputs and subtracts turnover-based trading costs before computing Sharpe.
